history_text: 54-year-old male with a 9-year history of Type 2 diabetes mellitus, currently poorly controlled (HbA1c 10.7%). Comorbidities include obstructive sleep apnoea, coronary artery disease, hypertension. Renal function is mildly to moderately reduced (eGFR 51.0 mL/min/1.73m²). Current medications include Insulin NPH 22 units nightly, Rosuvastatin 10mg nightly, Aspirin 81mg once daily. No known drug allergies. He denies recent hospitalisations or surgical procedures.
